title: Erdős solve portfolio: verifier-gated counterexample/witness search on finite-closure problems
doctrine: A 'solve' here = a finite object that closes a finite-closure Erdős problem (counterexample to a falsifiable claim, or witness to a verifiable existence claim), certified by a FROZEN cheap verifier and the Canopus gate, then submitted to erdosproblems.com. Record-movement is explicitly NOT a solve.

arms.claim: Alavi-Erdos-Malde-Schwenk 1987: the independence polynomial of every forest is unimodal.
arms.solve_event: a single tree/forest whose independence-polynomial coefficient sequence has a strict interior valley (DISPROVES).
arms.search: scripts/search_993_v3.py — exhaustive small trees + caterpillars + spiders + millions of random uniform trees.

arms.claim: No length-k>=4 coprime arithmetic progression has a perfect-power product (Erdos; d=1 is Erdos-Selfridge 1975).
arms.solve_event: a single (n,d,k) with gcd(n,d)=1, k>=4 whose product n(n+d)...(n+(k-1)d) is a perfect power (RESOLVES).
arms.search: scripts/verify_erdos672.py — bounded (n,d,k) sweep with exact integer perfect-power test.

arms.claim: Exists k>2 s.t. the k-subsets of {1..2k} are (k+1)-colourable with every (k+1)-set rainbow (equiv. chi(J(2k,k))<=k+1).
arms.solve_event: an explicit (k+1)-colouring for some k>2 (RESOLVES, YES). Small-k non-colourability is bounded evidence, not a solve.
arms.search: scripts/verify_erdos835.py — exact backtracking (k+1)-colouring of the Johnson graph J(2k,k).

arms.claim: For every prime p and finite A ⊆ F_p\{0}, some ordering of A has all partial sums distinct mod p.
arms.solve_event: a single (p,A) for which an EXHAUSTED backtracking search finds no valid ordering (RESOLVES: not always).
arms.search: scripts/verify_erdos475.py — exact backtracking sequenceability decision; full F_p*, exhaustive small-p subsets, random subsets.

arms.claim: Every run of consecutive composites n+1..n+k has distinct primes p_i | n+i (a prime SDR).
arms.solve_event: a maximal composite run whose prime-divisor bipartite graph has no SDR — a Hall-violating set (RESOLVES: not always).
arms.search: scripts/verify_erdos375.py — exact bipartite matching + Hall-violation certificate over maximal composite runs (SDR is hereditary, so maximal runs suffice).

decision_rule: For each arm, accept a SOLVE iff the frozen verifier validates the object AND the Canopus gate passes (>=2 independent verifications + surviving adversarial probe). No witness in the searched bound is banked as an honest null (bounded confirmation), never as a solve.
